input.content:
# Personal Task Manager

A simple, command-line task management system using the Fibonacci priority system (P0-P8) for effective prioritization.

## Features

- **Fibonacci Priority System**: P0 (critical) → P1 → P2 → P3 → P5 → P8 (low priority)
- **Task Status Tracking**: pending, in_progress, blocked, completed
- **Simple CLI Interface**: Easy commands for daily task management
- **JSON Storage**: Local file-based storage for portability
- **Statistics**: Track completion rates and priority distribution

## Quick Start

```bash
# Install dependencies
npm install

# Make executable (optional)
chmod +x task-manager.js

# Create your first task
node task-manager.js add "Review project requirements" "Go through all specs" P2

# List all tasks
node task-manager.js list

# Start working on a task
node task-manager.js start <task-id>

# Complete a task
node task-manager.js complete <task-id>

# View statistics
node task-manager.js stats
```

## Commands

- `add "title" [description] [priority]` - Create new task
- `list [status] [priority]` - List tasks with optional filters
- `complete <id>` - Mark task as completed
- `start <id>` - Set task status to in_progress
- `stats` - Show completion statistics
- `help` - Display help information

## Priority System

The Fibonacci sequence creates natural scarcity constraints:

- **P0**: System down, security breach, critical deadlines
- **P1**: Important meetings, client deliverables, blocking work
- **P2**: Planned sprint work, scheduled features
- **P3**: Routine development, documentation
- **P5**: Nice-to-have features, optimizations
- **P8**: Research, experimental work, someday/maybe

## Examples

```bash
# High priority bug fix
node task-manager.js add "Fix login authentication bug" "Users cannot log in" P1

# Regular development work
node task-manager.js add "Implement user profile page" "" P2

# Low priority improvement
node task-manager.js add "Optimize image loading" "Reduce page load times" P5

# List only pending tasks
node task-manager.js list pending

# List only P1 priority tasks
node task-manager.js list "" P1
```

## Task Data Structure

Tasks are stored in `tasks.json` with the following structure:

```json
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "title": "Task title",
  "description": "Optional description",
  "priority": "P0|P1|P2|P3|P5|P8",
  "status": "pending|in_progress|blocked|completed",
  "created_at": "ISO date",
  "updated_at": "ISO date",
  "completed_at": "ISO date or null",
  "estimated_hours": null,
  "actual_hours": null,
  "tags": []
}
```

## Integration

This task manager can be easily integrated with other systems:

- **Cron Jobs**: Automate task creation from external sources
- **Git Hooks**: Create tasks from commit messages or issues
- **Web Interface**: Build a simple web UI on top of the JSON storage
- **Reporting**: Generate reports from the task data

## Philosophy

Based on proven productivity patterns:
- **Fibonacci Priorities**: Enforce scarcity and prevent "everything is P1"
- **Simple Interface**: Reduce friction in task management
- **Local Storage**: Keep data under your control
- **Extensible**: Easy to modify and integrate with other tools

content.text:
I've created a complete personal task management system with the following key features:

## 🎯 **Core Features**
- **Fibonacci Priority System** (P0-P8) for natural prioritization constraints
- **Status Tracking** (pending → in_progress → completed/blocked)
- **CLI Interface** with simple commands (`add`, `list`, `complete`, `start`, `stats`)
- **JSON Storage** for portability and easy integration

## 📊 **Priority System**
The Fibonacci sequence (P0, P1, P2, P3, P5, P8) creates scarcity constraints that prevent "everything is high priority" syndrome:
- **P0**: Critical system issues
- **P1**: Important deadlines, blocking work  
- **P2**: Planned development work
- **P3**: Routine tasks
- **P5**: Nice-to-have features
- **P8**: Research and experimental work

## 🚀 **Quick Usage**
```bash
# Create tasks
node task-manager.js add "Fix login bug" "Authentication failing" P1

# List tasks (sorted by priority)
node task-manager.js list

# Work on a task
node task-manager.js start <task-id>

# Complete it
node task-manager.js complete <task-id>

# View statistics
node task-manager.js stats
```

The system automatically sorts tasks by priority and provides completion statistics. All data is stored locally in `tasks.json` for easy backup and portability. The CLI interface makes it perfect for developers who prefer command-line workflows.
